response: {"calibration_score":0.31,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":1,"well_calibrated_count":10,"biases_detected":["anchoring — repeated attempts on same .env.local path after initial failure without changing approach","availability — defaulted to shell heredoc for multi-line jq content before trying Write tool, causing two failed attempts","sunk-cost — continued sleep-based polling strategy in unit-24 after first Bash sleep command failed rather than switching to Monitor","path-assumption — assumed working directory context in several Bash calls (grep without abs path, npm test without cd) causing preventable failures"],"recommendations":["Use Write tool for multi-line file content instead of heredoc in Bash; heredoc quoting interacts badly with jq escape sequences and caused two failed attempts before Write succeeded","Replace sleep-poll patterns with Monitor tool for background task output; the 60s sleep in unit-24 failed and the same information was obtained synchronously via Read","After a tool call fails on a path, verify the path exists with Glob before retrying rather than retrying the same call or switching to a sibling path — units 20 and 22 made 3-4 probing calls that a single Glob would have collapsed","Run Bash commands with explicit absolute paths or leading cd when CWD is ambiguous; several units issued relative-path commands that failed because CWD was the parent repo rather than the frontend subdirectory"]}
